channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: At least 35 dead as tornadoes, wildfires, and blinding dust sweep across US
item.description: Violent tornadoes and high winds decimated homes, wiped out schools and toppled semitractor-trailers as a monster storm that also produced dust storms and icy conditions killed at least 35 people across the central and southern U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump orders strikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen and issues new warning
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said he ordered a series of airstrikes on the Houthi-held areas in Yemen on Saturday, promising to use “overwhelming lethal force” until the Iran-backed rebels cease their attacks on shipping along a vital maritime corridor.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, SAMY MAGDY and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Key witness in American college student's disappearance has his passport seized
item.description: Joshua Riibe, who was seen with University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki before she went missing, no longer has his passport, according to his attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SpaceX mission to replace stuck NASA astronauts launches
item.description: A Falcon 9 rocket carrying the Crew-10 successfully launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Monster storm across the US sparks threat of tornadoes and fire, killing at least 16
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — The threat of violent tornadoes in parts of the U.S. proved deadly as well as destructive as whipping winds moved east into the Mississippi Valley and Deep South on Saturday, with at least 16 people killed and scores of homes decimated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Another state looks to ban this common question drivers are asked every day
item.description: A bill has been introduced into the Colorado legislature that would prohibit officers from asking "Do you know why I pulled you over," and instead tell the driver why they are conducting the traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, Fox 5

item.title: Protestant denominations try new ideas as they face declines in members and money
item.description: When the Episcopal Church recently announced cuts to its national staff, it was the latest in a long-running cycle among historic U.S. Protestant denominations — declines in members leading to declines in funding and thus in staff. And it wasn't alone. The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma brought by the Biden administration’s Department of Justice, which challenged the states' immigration laws making it a crime for someone to be in the state if they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DHS agents search Columbia dorm rooms after Khalil arrest
item.description: Department of Homeland Security agents searched two dorm rooms at Columbia University Thursday night, the school said, a move that comes less than a week after the federal arrest of former grad student Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's tariff wars forge rare bipartisan alliance in Kentucky as bourbon makers fear escalation
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's blunt-force use of tariffs has forged a rare bipartisan alliance among Kentucky's top leaders — Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear and Republican Sens. Mitch McConnell and Rand Paul — in lamenting brewing trade wars that could stagger the bourbon sector.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department investigating dozens of universities after DEI warning letter
item.description: The Education Department announced Friday it is investigating more than 50 colleges and universities over what it called "racial preferences" in academics or scholarships, a move that comes amid the Trump administration's wider crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Newborn kittens rescued from wrecked car moments before demolition
item.description: A litter of kittens was rescued from the inside of a wrecked car at a junkyard moments before it was scheduled to be demolished Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID)] funding is now forcing us to wind down critical work here in Baltimore and internationally,” a spokesperson for the school said in an email.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration resumes immigrant family detentions
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants, a legal nonprofit said, reigniting a controversial practice largely curtailed under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Second judge blocks mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: A second federal judge has blocked the Trump administration from firing federal workers, issuing an order Thursday that probationary employees be reinstated weeks after their dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Founding girl group member reveals she's been living in her car
item.description: "If you would have said to me while I was En Vogue, 'You're going to be living in your car one day,' I'd be like, 'Huh?"
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: This common mixup is causing people to accidentally put glue in their eyes
item.description: Two very similar bottles stored in the same place in the home have led to a growing number of people seeking medical attention for accidentally putting glue in their eyes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FEMA launches review of migrant shelter aid, suggesting smuggling laws were violated
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration has launched a review of organizations that provide temporary housing and other aid to migrants, suggesting they may have violated a law used to prosecute smugglers.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump must reinstate many fired federal employees
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that the Trump administration must reinstate probationary government employees fired unlawfully at several agencies, lambasting the Justice Department at a hearing for a “sham” gambit that enabled a key official to avoid testifying in the case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pete Buttigieg declines Senate run in Michigan
item.description: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced he will not run for Senate in Michigan, keeping the door open for a potential presidential run in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WGN 9

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, Fox 5

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Columbia grad student's detention will stretch on as lawyers spar over Trump's plan to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil will remain detained in Louisiana until at least next week but can finally speak to lawyers while they fight the Trump administration’s plans to deport the Columbia University graduate student for his role in campus protests against Israel, a judge decided at a hearing
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man arrested in Utah nursing home may not be extradited for 1977 Hawaii murder
item.description: A man who was arrested at a Utah nursing home in connection to a 1970s Hawaii murder may be released, according to a proposed court motion.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Tijuana airport blockage enters third day
item.description: Residents claim AMLO gave them full ownership and title to their land, something they now say is being "stolen" by state and local officials.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration resumes detention of immigrant families after Biden-era pause
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration resumed family detention of immigrants last week in a South Texas facility after a Biden-era pause, a legal nonprofit group providing services to migrant families said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department layoffs gut its civil rights office, leaving discrimination cases in limbo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department’s civil rights branch is losing nearly half its staff in the Trump administration’s layoffs, effectively gutting an office that already faced a backlog of thousands of complaints from students and families across the nation.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge appears skeptical probationary firings were for performance
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge repeatedly sounded skeptical on Wednesday that the Trump administration’s mass firings of probationary federal workers were made by the government because the employees couldn’t do their jobs, saying the terminations appeared to be part of a larger goal. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EPA likely to move to further limit federal protections for wetlands
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday announced it will reconsider the reach of the nation’s bedrock clean water law and likely further limit the wetlands it covers, building on a Supreme Court decision two years ago that removed federal protections for significant areas.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors say Kohberger's defense will argue evidence was planted in Idaho student murders case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors say Bryan Kohberger’s defense team will argue at his murder trial that someone else could have planted a knife sheath with Kohberger’s DNA at the home where four University of Idaho students were killed in 2022.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Schools use AI to monitor kids, hoping to prevent violence. Our investigation found security risks
item.description: One student asked a search engine, “Why does my boyfriend hit me?” Another threatened suicide in an email to an unrequited love. A gay teen opened up in an online diary about struggles with homophobic parents, writing they just wanted to be themselves.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE BRYAN of The Seattle Times and SHARON LURYE of The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WGN 9
